title: Reading and Use of English - Part 8
description: Read the four texts about remote work. For questions 47 – 56, choose from the people (A – D).

texts.A:
### The Manager (Liam)
'Managing a team you can't see requires a total shift in mindset,' says Liam. [51]I had to learn to judge performance based purely on output rather than hours spent at a desk.[/51] Initially, I worried about a drop in productivity, but the opposite happened. [48]However, I do miss the spontaneous 'water-cooler' moments where the best ideas often used to emerge.[/47] Now, everything has to be a scheduled meeting, which feels a bit clinical. [55]I've also noticed that newer employees struggle more because they don't have that organic 'learning by osmosis'[/55] that happens in a physical office.
texts.B:
### The Freelancer (Sofia)
Sofia has worked from home for a decade. 'The biggest myth is that we work in our pajamas,' she laughs. [47]To stay sane, I've had to implement a strict routine where I physically leave the house after work to signal the end of the day.[/47] [53]Without that boundary, the lines between my professional and private life become dangerously blurred.[/53] I value my autonomy, but [49]the lack of a steady social circle can sometimes lead to a feeling of being 'erased' from the industry.[/49] You have to be twice as loud online to be noticed.
texts.C:
### The Parent (Mark)
For Mark, remote work was a life-changer for his family. [50]I used to spend three hours a day commuting, which was essentially a part-time job I wasn't getting paid for.[/50] Now, I can take my kids to school and be present for dinner. [52]The downside is the constant battle for a quiet space; my 'office' is often the kitchen table amidst domestic chaos.[/52] [56]I've realized that I'm actually working more hours now because I feel I have to prove I'm not just slacking off at home.[/56] It's a paradox of flexibility.
texts.D:
### The Tech-Optimist (Chloe)
Chloe believes offices are a relic of the past. [54]She argues that digital collaboration tools are actually more efficient than face-to-face meetings because they leave a searchable record of every decision.[/54] 'We waste less time on small talk and more on deep work,' she says. However, [49]she admits that digital communication can lack the nuance of body language, leading to occasional misunderstandings.[/49] [53]She suggests that the future isn't just 'home or office', but a nomadic approach[/53] where people work from wherever they feel most inspired that day.

questions.question: mention a specific ritual used to separate work from personal time?
questions.explanation: Sofia mentions a strict routine of physically leaving the house to signal the end of the day.

questions.question: regret the loss of unplanned creative interactions?
questions.explanation: Liam misses 'spontaneous water-cooler moments' where best ideas emerged.

questions.question: acknowledge that a lack of physical presence can lead to communication or visibility issues?
questions.explanation: Sofia mentions feeling 'erased' and Chloe (D) mentions the lack of body language nuance.

questions.question: view their previous travel to work as an unpaid waste of time?
questions.explanation: Mark describes his three-hour commute as a 'part-time job I wasn't getting paid for'.

questions.question: mention changing the criteria used to evaluate staff?
questions.explanation: Liam learned to judge performance based on 'output rather than hours at a desk'.

questions.question: complain about a lack of dedicated professional space?
questions.explanation: Mark mentions his office is 'the kitchen table amidst domestic chaos'.

questions.question: express concern about work intruding into personal life?
questions.explanation: Sofia mentions the lines becoming 'dangerously blurred' (Chloe D also mentions blurring the fixed location).

questions.question: argue that digital records provide a superior way of tracking progress?
questions.explanation: Chloe says tools are more efficient because they leave a 'searchable record of every decision'.

questions.question: point out that remote work is particularly difficult for inexperienced workers?
questions.explanation: Liam notices newer employees struggle without 'learning by osmosis'.

questions.question: admit to working harder due to a fear of being perceived as lazy?
questions.explanation: Mark feels he has to work more to 'prove I'm not just slacking off'.
